title: Grammitis geluensis
intro: Grammitis geluensis is a species of fern belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wound healing, fever reduction, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Grammitis geluensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Grammitis geluensis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Grammitis geluensis is classified under the kingdom Plantae, the division Pteridophyta, and the class Polypodiopsida. It belongs to the family Polypodiaceae and the order Polypodiales. The genus Grammitis is a group of ferns commonly known as button ferns or resin ferns. Within the Grammitis genus, Grammitis geluensis is a specific species that is native to certain regions.
